standard_description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
subject: Language

khan_exercises.title: Introduction to the possessive
khan_exercises.description: Learn to use apostrophes to indicate possession, or ownership -- and when not to use them.
topic_path: Punctuation: end-of-sentence punctuation, the comma, and the apostrophe

items.question_content:
**Pick the answer that correctly uses the possessive case in this sentence.**

That piece of pie is  \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
The **possessive case** allows us to show that one thing *belongs* to another. To form the possessive case, we simply take a noun and add an apostrophe + the letter `s`:

Noun | Possessive | Example
- | - | -
Benjamin | Benjamin's | Benjamin's guitar
week | week's | A week's time

**However,** when we're making a *pronoun* possessive, *we don't use an apostrophe:*

Pronoun | Possessive | Example
- | - | -
I/me | my/mine | my sister
he/him | his | his house
she/her | her/hers | the hat is hers
it | its | its entrance
they | their/theirs | their favorite restaurant
who | whose | whose car is this?
The correct possessive form is **hers**.

khan_exercises.title: Advanced (plural) possession
khan_exercises.description: Possessive plurals have a complicated relationship with apostrophes, but this exercise is here to help you understand it!
topic_path: Punctuation: end-of-sentence punctuation, the comma, and the apostrophe

items.question_content:
**Choose the option that correctly uses the apostrophe to show a plural possessive.**

The two \_\_\_\_\_\_ teams are rivals.


[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
To form the **possessive case** for a plural noun ending in `-s`, we simply place an apostrophe *after* the `s`:

Plural noun | Possessive case | Example
- | - | -
wolves | wolves' | the wolves' prey
Joneses | Joneses' | the Joneses' house
The correct possessive form is **schools'**.

Joneses | Joneses' | the Joneses' house
The correct possessive form is **Finches'**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the option that correctly uses the apostrophe to show a plural possessive.**

I canâ€™t find any clothes I like in the \_\_\_\_\_\_ section.


[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
To form the **possessive case** for a plural noun ending in `-s`, we simply place an apostrophe *after* the `s`:

Plural noun | Possessive case | Example
- | - | -
wolves | wolves' | the wolves' prey
Joneses | Joneses' | the Joneses' house

Since the plural noun "women" doesn't end in `-s`, we can form a normal possessive. 

The correct possessive form is **women's**.

hints:
To form the **possessive case** for a plural noun ending in `-s`, we simply place an apostrophe *after* the `s`:

Plural noun | Possessive case | Example
- | - | -
wolves | wolves' | the wolves' prey
Joneses | Joneses' | the Joneses' house

Since the plural noun "children" doesn't end in `-s`, we can form a normal possessive. 

The correct possessive form is **children's**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the option that correctly uses the apostrophe to show a plural possessive.**

I was supposed to water my \_\_\_\_\_\_ plants while they were out of town, but I forgot.
